front: What is the primary bottleneck when running UVM testbenches on emulators?
back: Software-to-hardware synchronization for every pin-level signal change. Each @(posedge clk) is a round-trip between the host CPU testbench and the emulated DUT hardware.

front: How does Transaction-Level Acceleration (TLA) improve emulation throughput?
back: TLA moves pin-wiggling logic into synthesized transactors inside the emulator. The testbench sends one API call per transaction instead of hundreds of pin-level changes, reducing sync overhead 10-50×.

front: Why can't $display statements exist in emulated DUT code?
back: $display is a simulation-only system task that is not synthesizable. The DUT partition runs on emulator hardware (FPGAs/custom chips) that can only execute synthesizable constructs.
